# Emerging Models

Recent open-weight additions that matter strategically, but do not justify a dedicated lab file yet.

Use this file as a watchlist and routing supplement, not as a full substitute for lab-family references.

## Covered Here

- Trinity Large / Mini / Nano (Arcee AI)
- MiMo-V2-Pro (Xiaomi)
- Sarvam 30B / 105B
- Tiny Aya (Cohere)

Models already routed to their main family files are not duplicated here: GLM-5, MiniMax M2.5, Kimi K2.5, Gemma 4, and Nemotron-Cascade 2 remain in their primary lab references.

## Snapshot Table

| Model | Approx. Specs | Why It Matters |
| ----- | -------------- | -------------- |
| Trinity Large | ~400B / 13B active, Apache 2.0, ~128k context | US open-weight frontier MoE alternative |
| Trinity Mini | 26B / 3B active | Smaller Trinity deployment path |
| Trinity Nano | 6B / 1B active | Lowest-cost Trinity path |
| MiMo-V2-Pro | Reasoning-focused open-weight release | New entrant in 2026 reasoning wave |
| Sarvam 30B / 105B | Multilingual Indian reasoning models | Important regional and multilingual coverage |
| Tiny Aya | ~3.35B multilingual compact model | Strong very-small multilingual option |

## Operational Guidance

- Use Trinity when you want a new US Apache-style frontier MoE alternative to DeepSeek / Qwen / Kimi.
- Use Tiny Aya when multilingual support matters more than absolute reasoning depth and VRAM is constrained.
- Track Sarvam when Indian language coverage or regional deployment matters.
- Treat MiMo-V2-Pro as a watchlist model until stronger public deployment patterns emerge.
